hero.description: Production-ready configs for Claude Code, Cursor, Copilot, Antigravity, and more. One place for every AI coding tool.

howItWorks.title: How it works
howItWorks.browseDesc: Find configs by framework, use case, or tags. Every config is reviewed for quality.
howItWorks.copyDesc: One-click copy. Drop it into your project as CLAUDE.md, .cursorrules, or any config file.
howItWorks.shareDesc: Submit your configs via GitHub PR. Free or premium — you set the price.

cta.title: Got a killer agent config?
cta.description: Share it with the community or sell it as a premium config. Your expertise, your rules, your revenue.

configs.empty: No configs match your filters. Try adjusting them.

configs.step3Desc: The AI agent will now follow the rules defined in this config.

submit.title: Submit a Config
submit.description: Share your AI agent configuration with the community.

onboarding.title: First time here?
onboarding.description: uumax is a marketplace for AI agent configuration files — the files that tell tools like Claude Code, Cursor, or Windsurf how to behave.

whatIs.title: What is an AI Agent Config?
whatIs.description: AI coding tools like Claude Code, Cursor, and Windsurf use configuration files (CLAUDE.md, .cursorrules, etc.) to understand your coding style, project rules, and workflow. A good config turns a generic AI into your personal senior developer.
whatIs.withoutDesc: Generic responses, no context about your project, repeated mistakes
whatIs.withDesc: Knows your stack, follows your conventions, writes code your way

contributors.title: Contributors
contributors.subtitle: The people behind the configs. Want to join? Submit your config via GitHub PR.

guide.title: Getting Started
guide.subtitle: Everything you need to know to get the most out of uumax in 5 minutes.
guide.whatDesc: uumax is a marketplace for AI agent configuration files. These are plain text files that tell your AI coding tool how to behave — what stack you use, how to name variables, when to write tests, and more. A good config turns a generic AI into a developer who knows your project.
guide.step1Title: Step 1: Find a config for your tool
guide.step1Desc: Go to Browse and filter by your AI tool. Each config is labeled with the framework it’s for.
guide.step2Title: Step 2: Copy and save the config
guide.step2Desc: Click "Copy Config" on any config page. Then save it to your project with the correct filename for your tool.
guide.step3Desc: That’s it. Your AI tool will read the config automatically on the next session.
guide.toolTableTitle: Where to save the config file
guide.aiToolsDesc: uumax includes four free AI-powered tools. They all require an OpenRouter API key (free at openrouter.ai/keys).
guide.generatorDesc: Describe your ideal AI agent in plain English and get a production-ready config generated for you.
guide.remixDesc: Pick an existing config as a base, describe your customizations, and get a tailored version. Also lets you export the result as any tool’s config file.
guide.healthDesc: Paste any config and get an AI review with scores, specific issues, and suggested improvements.
guide.compareDesc: Pick two configs for a side-by-side AI analysis of differences, strengths, and which to use when.
guide.apiKeyDesc: Click "Try it" on any config page. Enter your OpenRouter API key (starts with sk-or-). The key is stored in your browser only — never sent to uumax servers.
guide.skillsDesc: Skills are MCP servers — extensions that let your AI agent interact with the outside world: read files, search the web, query databases, manage GitHub issues. MCP is supported by Claude Code, Cursor, Continue.dev, Windsurf, and Google Antigravity.
guide.skillsStep1: Go to Skills and find a skill you want
guide.skillsStep2: Copy the install command or follow the setup guide
guide.skillsStep3: Add it to your tool’s MCP config file and restart
guide.faq1Q: Why is the Generate button grayed out?
guide.faq1A: You need to fill in the description field above the button first.
guide.faq2A: No. Everything is free to browse and copy. No login required.
guide.faq3Q: Can I use the same config with multiple tools?
guide.faq3A: Yes. Use the Remix tool to export any config in 5 different formats (CLAUDE.md, .cursorrules, copilot-instructions.md, etc.) at once.
guide.faq4Q: How do I submit my own config?
guide.faq4A: Click Submit in the nav, or open a PR directly on GitHub. Free and premium configs are both welcome.
guide.faq5Q: Do Skills (MCPs) work with all tools?
guide.faq5A: MCP is supported by Claude Code, Cursor, Continue.dev, Windsurf, and Google Antigravity. OpenAI tools (GPT Codex) use a different protocol and are not compatible.
